title: ericameria nauseosa var. washoensis
intro: Ericameria nauseosa var. washoensis, also known as Washtucha or Wasco paintbrush, is a shrub plant that belongs to the sunflower family Asteraceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Ericameria nauseosa var. washoensis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the plant's leaves and stems in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
